Cache lines from Python source files.
This is intended to read lines from modules imported -- hence if a filename
is not found, it will look down the module search path for a file by
that name.
getline
clearcache
checkcache
	lazycachec
Clear the cache entirely.N)
cache
clear
!/usr/lib64/python3.9/linecache.pyr
z~Get a line for a Python source file from the cache.
    Update the cache if it doesn't contain an entry for this file already.
getlines
len)
filename
lineno
module_globals
linesr
Get the lines for a Python source file from the cache.
    Update the cache if it doesn't contain an entry for this file already.r
updatecache
MemoryErrorr
entryr
rHq.|
r^q.z
zUDiscard cache entries that are out of date.
    (This is not checked upon each call!)Nr
listr
keysr
stat
OSError
st_size
st_mtime)
	filenamesr
size
mtimer
fullnamer
Update a cache entry and return its list of lines.
    If something's wrong, print a message, discard the cache entry,
    and return an empty list.r
liner
<listcomp>p
updatecache.<locals>.<listcomp>r
startswith
endswithr
ImportError
splitlines
path
isabs
join
	TypeError
AttributeError
tokenize
open
	readlinesr
basename
data
dirname
Seed the cache for filename with module_globals.
    The module loader will be asked for the source only when getlines is
    called, not immediately.
    If there is an entry in the cache already, it is not altered.
    :return: True if a lazy load is registered in the cache,
        otherwise False. To register such a load a module loader with a
        get_source method must be found, the filename must be a cacheable
        filename, and the filename must not be already cached.
    r
TFr"
__loader__
__name__
get_sourceN)
getattr
	functools
partial)
name
loaderr=
	get_linesr
__doc__r@
__all__r
<module>
